title: Feature-group drift decomposition with per-group severity and narrative

observation.what_i_noticed: The drift detector reports 89.2% of features as drifting (severity: critical), but this flat number is not actionable. In a prolonged bull market, momentum and volatility features WILL naturally drift from their historical distributions — this is expected, not alarming. The critical severity causes the crash model weight to be reduced to 20%, which may be overly conservative when the drift is concentrated in low-importance feature groups while macro/credit indicators remain stable.
observation.root_cause: The drift detector treats all features equally in its raw severity classification and provides no structural breakdown of WHERE drift is occurring. Users and downstream systems see '141/158 features drifted' with no way to distinguish expected drift (momentum in a bull run) from concerning drift (macro indicators shifting toward recession).

what_i_did.summary: Added feature-group drift decomposition that classifies 158 features into 10 semantic groups (momentum, volatility, tail_risk, price_distance, technical, vix, credit_yields, cross_asset, macro, interaction) and reports per-group drift metrics including drift_pct, mean_psi, top drifted features, and importance weight. Also generates a human-readable narrative summarizing which groups are drifting and which are stable.
what_i_did.approach: Pattern-based feature classification using config-driven prefix matching. Groups are defined in config.py so they can be tuned without code changes. The decomposition plugs into existing check_drift() and from_rolling_window() methods, and the drift router exposes group_drift and drift_narrative in the API response.
key_design_decisions: Interaction features (_x_) checked first since they span groups (vol_x_mom would otherwise match momentum)
Group patterns stored in config.py for tunability
Per-group importance_weight included when crash model importances are available
Narrative is a concise one-sentence summary, not a verbose report
All changes are additive — no existing report keys modified

categories: TestFeatureClassification (9 tests): verifies each group's features are correctly classified
TestGroupDriftSummary (6 tests): per-group structure, drift_pct correctness, importance weights

before.drift_output: Flat: 141/158 features drifted, severity=critical, list of 10 feature names
before.actionability: Low — user sees 'critical' but can't tell what's actually concerning

after.drift_output: Structured: per-group breakdown (momentum: 100% drifted, macro: 10% drifted, etc.), importance weights per group, narrative summary
after.actionability: High — user can see 'momentum and volatility are drifting (expected in bull market) but macro indicators are stable — model reliability is moderate'

analysis.impact: Medium-high. The drift signal goes from a blunt 'critical' warning to a structured decomposition that enables smarter decision-making. Frontend can eventually render per-group drift bars. The narrative makes the API self-documenting.
limitations: Pattern matching is prefix-based, not regex — sufficient for current feature names but may need updating if naming conventions change
Narrative is simple (drifting vs stable) — doesn't yet say 'expected in bull markets'
Group severity thresholds are hardcoded in _build_narrative (60%/20%) — could be config-driven
analysis.what_went_well: Clean additive change — no existing tests broken, no existing API contracts modified. The interaction feature ordering bug was caught immediately by tests.
analysis.what_could_be_better: Could have added regime-conditional narrative (e.g., 'momentum drift is expected in a 2-year bull run') but this requires coupling drift_detector to regime_detector which adds complexity.

next_steps: Add regime-conditional drift interpretation (momentum drift in bull = expected, macro drift in bull = concerning)
Frontend visualization: per-group drift bars on the drift dashboard
Use group-level drift to compute a more nuanced effective_severity (e.g., if only momentum/vol drift but macro is stable, cap severity at 'moderate')
Add group_drift to the engine data collection for lab cycle tracking
